Haiden | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 所在地={{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}{{jalink|Sōsa|Q858367}} Oio 75 | 祭神=Asahiko-no-Mikoto | 別名=Sōsa Daimyōjin | 創建={{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} 7 | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|August 15|Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Oio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oio Shrine''' (おいおじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Oio, {{jalink|Sōsa District|Q7667621}}, {{jalink|Shimōsa Province|Q1053695}} (present-day {{jalink|Sōsa|Q858367}}, {{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}). It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small shrine), and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{jalink|Asahiko-no-Mikoto}}, child deity of {{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}, deity of {{jalink|Katori Jingū|Q372380}}. Also enshrined are {{jalink|Iwatsutsuno-o|Q9010200|Iwatsutsuno-o-no-Mikoto and Iwatsutsuno-me-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|Q478504|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto}}. There is also a theory that {{jalink|Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689}} is enshrined. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in year 7 of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}. It is listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as “Oio Shrine, {{jalink|Sōsa District|Q7667621}}, {{jalink|Shimōsa Province|Q1053695}},” and ranked as a small shrine. Within {{jalink|Shimōsa Province|Q1053695}}, the only {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} in the territory of the {{jalink|Shimotsu-unakami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11361123}} besides this shrine was {{jalink|Katori Jingū|Q372380}}, and this shrine was treated as the {{jalink|sōsha}} of Sōsa District in Shimōsa. The district where the shrine is located, {{jalink|Sōsa District|Q7667621}}, is said to have been established due to the military merits of {{jalink|Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689}} in subduing the {{jalink|Bandō}} region; traditions also convey deep ties with the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}}, including three generations of the {{jalink|Mononobe Sōsa clan|Q24864171|Mononobe no Sōsa clan}} - {{jalink|Mononobe no Sōsa no Ashitsugi|Q11570688|Ashitsugi}}, {{jalink|Mononobe no Sōsa no Kuma-i|Q11570687|Kuma-i}}, and Suemori - serving as commanders guarding the region, together with {{jalink|Shita District|Q11386034}} in {{jalink|Hitachi Province|Q1055353}}. Also, {{jalink|Sōsa Shrine}} exists as a {{jalink|sessha|Q135009904}} of {{jalink|Katori Jingū|Q372380}}, and rebuilding this {{jalink|subsidiary shrine|Q1071160|sessha}} is said to have formerly been a duty of Sōsa District. Furthermore, this shrine’s priestly family was the {{jalink|Katori clan}}, and {{jalink|Katori no Muraji Ioshima}}, who was granted {{jalink|external rank|Q11430321|external}} {{jalink|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} for contributions of private grain to the garrison in {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}, is said either to have lived in Sōsa District or to have retired there after stepping down as {{jalink|negi|Q11594490|chief negi}} in old age. These traditions show ties with {{jalink|Katori Jingū|Q372380}}, and ties between Katori Jingū and the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}} are also discussedKenichi Tanigawa, ''Nihon no Kamigami: Jinja to Seichi 11'', Hakusuisha, 2000, ISBN 4-560-02511-8, pp.265-267. In {{jalink|Shōhei (Japan)|Q1143587|Shōhei}} 24 ({{jalink|1369|Q6211}}), the shrine buildings were burned down and rebuilt by the {{jalink|Chiba clan|Q2975237}}. Thereafter, it was protected by the Chiba clan, but declined along with that clan’s weakening. In October {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Precincts === Behind the shrine buildings on the left is a sacred cedar designated by the city as a {{jalink|natural monument|Q23790}}. === Access === * {{jalink|JR East|Q499071}} / {{jalink|Sōbu Main Line|Q1191364}}, get off at {{jalink|Yōkaichiba Station|Q5363766}}, then 25 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おいおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oio Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oio Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:匝瑳市の建築物]] [[Category:匝瑳市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県匝瑳市生尾75', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:老尾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610638}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Oio Shrine | 画像=[[File:老尾神社 拝殿.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}{{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367}} Oio 75 | 祭神=Asahiko-no-Mikoto | 別名=Sōsa Daimyōjin | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} 7 | 社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Oio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oio Shrine''' (おいおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Oio, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}). It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small shrine), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill||lt=Asahiko-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, child deity of {{ill||lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, deity of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}. Also enshrined are {{ill|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o|Q9010200|WD=Q9010200|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o-no-Mikoto and Iwatsutsuno-me-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|Q478504|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto}}. There is also a theory that {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689}} is enshrined. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Oio Shrine, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}},” and ranked as a small shrine. Within {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}}, the only {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} in the territory of the {{ill|lt=Shimotsu-unakami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11361123|WD=Q11361123}} besides this shrine was {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and this shrine was treated as the {{ill||lt=sōsha|UNKNOWN}} of Sōsa District in Shimōsa. The district where the shrine is located, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, is said to have been established due to the military merits of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689}} in subduing the {{ill||lt=Bandō|UNKNOWN}} region; traditions also convey deep ties with the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}, including three generations of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe Sōsa clan|Q24864171|WD=Q24864171|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa clan}} - {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Ashitsugi|Q11570688|WD=Q11570688|lt=Ashitsugi}}, {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Kuma-i|Q11570687|WD=Q11570687|lt=Kuma-i}}, and Suemori - serving as commanders guarding the region, together with {{ill|lt=Shita District|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}}. Also, {{ill||lt=Sōsa Shrine|UNKNOWN}} exists as a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904}} of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and rebuilding this {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrine|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha}} is said to have formerly been a duty of Sōsa District. Furthermore, this shrine’s priestly family was the {{ill||lt=Katori clan|UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill||lt=Katori no Muraji Ioshima|UNKNOWN}}, who was granted {{ill|lt=external rank|Q11430321|WD=Q11430321|lt=external}} {{ill||lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|UNKNOWN}} for contributions of private grain to the garrison in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}, is said either to have lived in Sōsa District or to have retired there after stepping down as {{ill|lt=negi|Q11594490|WD=Q11594490|lt=chief negi}} in old age. These traditions show ties with {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and ties between Katori Jingū and the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}} are also discussedKenichi Tanigawa, ''Nihon no Kamigami: Jinja to Seichi 11'', Hakusuisha, 2000, ISBN 4-560-02511-8, pp.265-267. In {{ill|lt=Shōhei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei}} 24 ({{ill|lt=1369|Q6211|WD=Q6211}}), the shrine buildings were burned down and rebuilt by the {{ill|lt=Chiba clan|Q2975237|WD=Q2975237}}. Thereafter, it was protected by the Chiba clan, but declined along with that clan’s weakening. In October {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precincts === Behind the shrine buildings on the left is a sacred cedar designated by the city as a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}}. === Access === * {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071}} / {{ill|lt=Sōbu Main Line|Q1191364|WD=Q1191364}}, get off at {{ill|lt=Yōkaichiba Station|Q5363766|WD=Q5363766}}, then 25 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おいおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oio Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oio Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:匝瑳市の建築物]] [[Category:匝瑳市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県匝瑳市生尾75', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:老尾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610638}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Oio Shrine | 画像=[[File:老尾神社 拝殿.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}{{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367}} Oio 75 | 祭神=Asahiko-no-Mikoto | 別名=Sōsa Daimyōjin | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} 7 | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Oio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oio Shrine''' (おいおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Oio, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small shrine), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahiko-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, child deity of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, deity of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}. Also enshrined are {{ill|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o|Q9010200|WD=Q9010200|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o-no-Mikoto and Iwatsutsuno-me-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|Q478504|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto}}. There is also a theory that {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689}} is enshrined. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Oio Shrine, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}},” and ranked as a small shrine. Within {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}}, the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} in the territory of the {{ill|lt=Shimotsu-unakami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11361123|WD=Q11361123}} besides this shrine was {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and this shrine was treated as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|UNKNOWN}} of Sōsa District in Shimōsa. The district where the shrine is located, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, is said to have been established due to the military merits of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689}} in subduing the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bandō|UNKNOWN}} region; traditions also convey deep ties with the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}, including three generations of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe Sōsa clan|Q24864171|WD=Q24864171|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa clan}} - {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Ashitsugi|Q11570688|WD=Q11570688|lt=Ashitsugi}}, {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Kuma-i|Q11570687|WD=Q11570687|lt=Kuma-i}}, and Suemori - serving as commanders guarding the region, together with {{ill|lt=Shita District|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}}. Also, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōsa Shrine|UNKNOWN}} exists as a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904}} of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and rebuilding this {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrine|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha}} is said to have formerly been a duty of Sōsa District. Furthermore, this shrine’s priestly family was the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori clan|UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori no Muraji Ioshima|UNKNOWN}}, who was granted {{ill|lt=external rank|Q11430321|WD=Q11430321|lt=external}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|UNKNOWN}} for contributions of private grain to the garrison in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}, is said either to have lived in Sōsa District or to have retired there after stepping down as {{ill|lt=negi|Q11594490|WD=Q11594490|lt=chief negi}} in old age. These traditions show ties with {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and ties between Katori Jingū and the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}} are also discussedKenichi Tanigawa, ''Nihon no Kamigami: Jinja to Seichi 11'', Hakusuisha, 2000, ISBN 4-560-02511-8, pp.265-267. In {{ill|lt=Shōhei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei}} 24 ({{ill|lt=1369|Q6211|WD=Q6211}}), the shrine buildings were burned down and rebuilt by the {{ill|lt=Chiba clan|Q2975237|WD=Q2975237}}. Thereafter, it was protected by the Chiba clan, but declined along with that clan’s weakening. In October {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precincts === Behind the shrine buildings on the left is a sacred cedar designated by the city as a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}}. === Access === * {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071}} / {{ill|lt=Sōbu Main Line|Q1191364|WD=Q1191364}}, get off at {{ill|lt=Yōkaichiba Station|Q5363766|WD=Q5363766}}, then 25 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おいおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oio Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oio Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:匝瑳市の建築物]] [[Category:匝瑳市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県匝瑳市生尾75', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:老尾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610638}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Oio Shrine | 画像=[[File:老尾神社 拝殿.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}{{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367}} Oio 75 | 祭神=Asahiko-no-Mikoto | 別名=Sōsa Daimyōjin | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} 7 | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Oio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oio Shrine''' (おいおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Oio, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small shrine), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, child deity of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, deity of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}. Also enshrined are {{ill|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o|Q9010200|WD=Q9010200|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o-no-Mikoto and Iwatsutsuno-me-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|Q478504|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto}}. There is also a theory that {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689}} is enshrined. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Oio Shrine, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}},” and ranked as a small shrine. Within {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}}, the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} in the territory of the {{ill|lt=Shimotsu-unakami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11361123|WD=Q11361123}} besides this shrine was {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and this shrine was treated as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} of Sōsa District in Shimōsa. The district where the shrine is located, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, is said to have been established due to the military merits of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689}} in subduing the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bandō|1=UNKNOWN}} region; traditions also convey deep ties with the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}, including three generations of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe Sōsa clan|Q24864171|WD=Q24864171|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa clan}} - {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Ashitsugi|Q11570688|WD=Q11570688|lt=Ashitsugi}}, {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Kuma-i|Q11570687|WD=Q11570687|lt=Kuma-i}}, and Suemori - serving as commanders guarding the region, together with {{ill|lt=Shita District|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}}. Also, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōsa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} exists as a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904}} of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and rebuilding this {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrine|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha}} is said to have formerly been a duty of Sōsa District. Furthermore, this shrine’s priestly family was the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori no Muraji Ioshima|1=UNKNOWN}}, who was granted {{ill|lt=external rank|Q11430321|WD=Q11430321|lt=external}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|1=UNKNOWN}} for contributions of private grain to the garrison in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}, is said either to have lived in Sōsa District or to have retired there after stepping down as {{ill|lt=negi|Q11594490|WD=Q11594490|lt=chief negi}} in old age. These traditions show ties with {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and ties between Katori Jingū and the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}} are also discussedKenichi Tanigawa, ''Nihon no Kamigami: Jinja to Seichi 11'', Hakusuisha, 2000, ISBN 4-560-02511-8, pp.265-267. In {{ill|lt=Shōhei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei}} 24 ({{ill|lt=1369|Q6211|WD=Q6211}}), the shrine buildings were burned down and rebuilt by the {{ill|lt=Chiba clan|Q2975237|WD=Q2975237}}. Thereafter, it was protected by the Chiba clan, but declined along with that clan’s weakening. In October {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precincts === Behind the shrine buildings on the left is a sacred cedar designated by the city as a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}}. === Access === * {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071}} / {{ill|lt=Sōbu Main Line|Q1191364|WD=Q1191364}}, get off at {{ill|lt=Yōkaichiba Station|Q5363766|WD=Q5363766}}, then 25 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おいおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oio Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oio Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:匝瑳市の建築物]] [[Category:匝瑳市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県匝瑳市生尾75', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:老尾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610638}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Oio Shrine | 画像=[[File:老尾神社 拝殿.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}{{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367}} Oio 75 | 祭神=Asahiko-no-Mikoto | 別名=Sōsa Daimyōjin | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} 7 | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Oio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oio Shrine''' (おいおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Oio, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small shrine), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, child deity of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, deity of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}. Also enshrined are {{ill|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o|Q9010200|WD=Q9010200|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o-no-Mikoto and Iwatsutsuno-me-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|Q478504|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto}}. There is also a theory that {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689}} is enshrined. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Oio Shrine, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}},” and ranked as a small shrine. Within {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}}, the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} in the territory of the {{ill|lt=Shimotsu-unakami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11361123|WD=Q11361123}} besides this shrine was {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and this shrine was treated as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} of Sōsa District in Shimōsa. The district where the shrine is located, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, is said to have been established due to the military merits of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689}} in subduing the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bandō|1=UNKNOWN}} region; traditions also convey deep ties with the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}, including three generations of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe Sōsa clan|Q24864171|WD=Q24864171|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa clan}} - {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Ashitsugi|Q11570688|WD=Q11570688|lt=Ashitsugi}}, {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Kuma-i|Q11570687|WD=Q11570687|lt=Kuma-i}}, and Suemori - serving as commanders guarding the region, together with {{ill|lt=Shita District|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}}. Also, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōsa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} exists as a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904}} of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and rebuilding this {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrine|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha}} is said to have formerly been a duty of Sōsa District. Furthermore, this shrine’s priestly family was the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori no Muraji Ioshima|1=UNKNOWN}}, who was granted {{ill|lt=external rank|Q11430321|WD=Q11430321|lt=external}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|1=UNKNOWN}} for contributions of private grain to the garrison in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}, is said either to have lived in Sōsa District or to have retired there after stepping down as {{ill|lt=negi|Q11594490|WD=Q11594490|lt=chief negi}} in old age. These traditions show ties with {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and ties between Katori Jingū and the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}} are also discussedKenichi Tanigawa, ''Nihon no Kamigami: Jinja to Seichi 11'', Hakusuisha, 2000, ISBN 4-560-02511-8, pp.265-267. In {{ill|lt=Shōhei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei}} 24 ({{ill|lt=1369|Q6211|WD=Q6211}}), the shrine buildings were burned down and rebuilt by the {{ill|lt=Chiba clan|Q2975237|WD=Q2975237}}. Thereafter, it was protected by the Chiba clan, but declined along with that clan’s weakening. In October {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precincts === Behind the shrine buildings on the left is a sacred cedar designated by the city as a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}}. === Access === * {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071}} / {{ill|lt=Sōbu Main Line|Q1191364|WD=Q1191364}}, get off at {{ill|lt=Yōkaichiba Station|Q5363766|WD=Q5363766}}, then 25 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おいおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oio Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oio Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:匝瑳市の建築物]] [[Category:匝瑳市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県匝瑳市生尾75', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:老尾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610638}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Oio Shrine | 画像=[[File:老尾神社 拝殿.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}{{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367}} Oio 75 | 祭神=Asahiko-no-Mikoto | 別名=Sōsa Daimyōjin | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} 7 | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Oio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oio Shrine''' (おいおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Oio, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small shrine), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, child deity of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}}, deity of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}. Also enshrined are {{ill|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o|Q9010200|WD=Q9010200|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o-no-Mikoto and Iwatsutsuno-me-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|Q478504|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto}}. There is also a theory that {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689}} is enshrined. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Oio Shrine, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}},” and ranked as a small shrine. Within {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}}, the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} in the territory of the {{ill|lt=Shimotsu-unakami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11361123|WD=Q11361123}} besides this shrine was {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and this shrine was treated as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} of Sōsa District in Shimōsa. The district where the shrine is located, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, is said to have been established due to the military merits of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689}} in subduing the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bandō|1=UNKNOWN}} region; traditions also convey deep ties with the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}, including three generations of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe Sōsa clan|Q24864171|WD=Q24864171|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa clan}} - {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Ashitsugi|Q11570688|WD=Q11570688|lt=Ashitsugi}}, {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Kuma-i|Q11570687|WD=Q11570687|lt=Kuma-i}}, and Suemori - serving as commanders guarding the region, together with {{ill|lt=Shita District|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}}. Also, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōsa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} exists as a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904}} of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and rebuilding this {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrine|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha}} is said to have formerly been a duty of Sōsa District. Furthermore, this shrine’s priestly family was the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori no Muraji Ioshima|1=UNKNOWN}}, who was granted {{ill|lt=external rank|Q11430321|WD=Q11430321|lt=external}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|1=UNKNOWN}} for contributions of private grain to the garrison in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}, is said either to have lived in Sōsa District or to have retired there after stepping down as {{ill|lt=negi|Q11594490|WD=Q11594490|lt=chief negi}} in old age. These traditions show ties with {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and ties between Katori Jingū and the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}} are also discussedKenichi Tanigawa, ''Nihon no Kamigami: Jinja to Seichi 11'', Hakusuisha, 2000, ISBN 4-560-02511-8, pp.265-267. In {{ill|lt=Shōhei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei}} 24 ({{ill|lt=1369|Q6211|WD=Q6211}}), the shrine buildings were burned down and rebuilt by the {{ill|lt=Chiba clan|Q2975237|WD=Q2975237}}. Thereafter, it was protected by the Chiba clan, but declined along with that clan’s weakening. In October {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precincts === Behind the shrine buildings on the left is a sacred cedar designated by the city as a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}}. === Access === * {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071}} / {{ill|lt=Sōbu Main Line|Q1191364|WD=Q1191364}}, get off at {{ill|lt=Yōkaichiba Station|Q5363766|WD=Q5363766}}, then 25 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おいおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oio Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oio Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:匝瑳市の建築物]] [[Category:匝瑳市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県匝瑳市生尾75', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:老尾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610638}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Oio Shrine | 画像=[[File:老尾神社 拝殿.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}{{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367}} Oio 75 | 祭神=Asahiko-no-Mikoto | 別名=Sōsa Daimyōjin | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} 7 | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Oio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oio Shrine''' (おいおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Oio, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small shrine), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, child deity of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}}, deity of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}. Also enshrined are {{ill|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o|Q9010200|WD=Q9010200|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o-no-Mikoto and Iwatsutsuno-me-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|Q478504|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto}}. There is also a theory that {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689}} is enshrined. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Oio Shrine, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}},” and ranked as a small shrine. Within {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}}, the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} in the territory of the {{ill|lt=Shimotsu-unakami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11361123|WD=Q11361123}} besides this shrine was {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and this shrine was treated as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} of Sōsa District in Shimōsa. The district where the shrine is located, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, is said to have been established due to the military merits of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689}} in subduing the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bandō|1=UNKNOWN}} region; traditions also convey deep ties with the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}, including three generations of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe Sōsa clan|Q24864171|WD=Q24864171|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa clan}} - {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Ashitsugi|Q11570688|WD=Q11570688|lt=Ashitsugi}}, {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Kuma-i|Q11570687|WD=Q11570687|lt=Kuma-i}}, and Suemori - serving as commanders guarding the region, together with {{ill|lt=Shita District|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}}. Also, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōsa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} exists as a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904}} of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and rebuilding this {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrine|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha}} is said to have formerly been a duty of Sōsa District. Furthermore, this shrine’s priestly family was the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori no Muraji Ioshima|1=UNKNOWN}}, who was granted {{ill|lt=external rank|Q11430321|WD=Q11430321|lt=external}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|1=UNKNOWN}} for contributions of private grain to the garrison in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}, is said either to have lived in Sōsa District or to have retired there after stepping down as {{ill|lt=negi|Q11594490|WD=Q11594490|lt=chief negi}} in old age. These traditions show ties with {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and ties between Katori Jingū and the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}} are also discussedKenichi Tanigawa, ''Nihon no Kamigami: Jinja to Seichi 11'', Hakusuisha, 2000, ISBN 4-560-02511-8, pp.265-267. In {{ill|lt=Shōhei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei}} 24 ({{ill|lt=1369|Q6211|WD=Q6211}}), the shrine buildings were burned down and rebuilt by the {{ill|lt=Chiba clan|Q2975237|WD=Q2975237}}. Thereafter, it was protected by the Chiba clan, but declined along with that clan’s weakening. In October {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precincts === Behind the shrine buildings on the left is a sacred cedar designated by the city as a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}}. === Access === * {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071}} / {{ill|lt=Sōbu Main Line|Q1191364|WD=Q1191364}}, get off at {{ill|lt=Yōkaichiba Station|Q5363766|WD=Q5363766}}, then 25 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おいおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oio Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oio Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:匝瑳市の建築物]] [[Category:匝瑳市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県匝瑳市生尾75', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:老尾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610638}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Oio Shrine | 画像=[[File:老尾神社 拝殿.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}{{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367}} Oio 75 | 祭神=Asahiko-no-Mikoto | 別名=Sōsa Daimyōjin | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} 7 | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Oio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oio Shrine''' (おいおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Oio, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small shrine), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, child deity of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}}, deity of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}. Also enshrined are {{ill|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o|Q9010200|WD=Q9010200|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o-no-Mikoto and Iwatsutsuno-me-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|Q478504|WD=Q478504|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto}}. There is also a theory that {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689}} is enshrined. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Oio Shrine, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}},” and ranked as a small shrine. Within {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}}, the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} in the territory of the {{ill|lt=Shimotsu-unakami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11361123|WD=Q11361123}} besides this shrine was {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and this shrine was treated as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} of Sōsa District in Shimōsa. The district where the shrine is located, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, is said to have been established due to the military merits of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689}} in subduing the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bandō|1=UNKNOWN}} region; traditions also convey deep ties with the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}, including three generations of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe Sōsa clan|Q24864171|WD=Q24864171|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa clan}} - {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Ashitsugi|Q11570688|WD=Q11570688|lt=Ashitsugi}}, {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Kuma-i|Q11570687|WD=Q11570687|lt=Kuma-i}}, and Suemori - serving as commanders guarding the region, together with {{ill|lt=Shita District|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}}. Also, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōsa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} exists as a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904}} of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and rebuilding this {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrine|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha}} is said to have formerly been a duty of Sōsa District. Furthermore, this shrine’s priestly family was the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori no Muraji Ioshima|1=UNKNOWN}}, who was granted {{ill|lt=external rank|Q11430321|WD=Q11430321|lt=external}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|1=UNKNOWN}} for contributions of private grain to the garrison in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}, is said either to have lived in Sōsa District or to have retired there after stepping down as {{ill|lt=negi|Q11594490|WD=Q11594490|lt=chief negi}} in old age. These traditions show ties with {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and ties between Katori Jingū and the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}} are also discussedKenichi Tanigawa, ''Nihon no Kamigami: Jinja to Seichi 11'', Hakusuisha, 2000, ISBN 4-560-02511-8, pp.265-267. In {{ill|lt=Shōhei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei}} 24 ({{ill|lt=1369|Q6211|WD=Q6211}}), the shrine buildings were burned down and rebuilt by the {{ill|lt=Chiba clan|Q2975237|WD=Q2975237}}. Thereafter, it was protected by the Chiba clan, but declined along with that clan’s weakening. In October {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precincts === Behind the shrine buildings on the left is a sacred cedar designated by the city as a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}}. === Access === * {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071}} / {{ill|lt=Sōbu Main Line|Q1191364|WD=Q1191364}}, get off at {{ill|lt=Yōkaichiba Station|Q5363766|WD=Q5363766}}, then 25 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おいおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oio Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oio Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:匝瑳市の建築物]] [[Category:匝瑳市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県匝瑳市生尾75', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:老尾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610638}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Oio Shrine | 画像=[[File:老尾神社 拝殿.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}{{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367}} Oio 75 | 祭神=Asahiko-no-Mikoto | 別名=Sōsa Daimyōjin | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} 7 | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Oio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oio Shrine''' (おいおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Oio, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small shrine), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, child deity of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}}, deity of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}. Also enshrined are {{ill|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o|Q9010200|WD=Q9010200|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o-no-Mikoto and Iwatsutsuno-me-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|Q478504|WD=Q478504|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto}}. There is also a theory that {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689}} is enshrined. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Oio Shrine, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}},” and ranked as a small shrine. Within {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}}, the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} in the territory of the {{ill|lt=Shimotsu-unakami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11361123|WD=Q11361123}} besides this shrine was {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and this shrine was treated as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} of Sōsa District in Shimōsa. The district where the shrine is located, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621}}, is said to have been established due to the military merits of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689}} in subduing the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bandō|1=UNKNOWN}} region; traditions also convey deep ties with the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}, including three generations of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe Sōsa clan|Q24864171|WD=Q24864171|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa clan}} - {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Ashitsugi|Q11570688|WD=Q11570688|lt=Ashitsugi}}, {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Kuma-i|Q11570687|WD=Q11570687|lt=Kuma-i}}, and Suemori - serving as commanders guarding the region, together with {{ill|lt=Shita District|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}}. Also, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōsa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} exists as a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904}} of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and rebuilding this {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrine|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha}} is said to have formerly been a duty of Sōsa District. Furthermore, this shrine’s priestly family was the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori no Muraji Ioshima|1=UNKNOWN}}, who was granted {{ill|lt=external rank|Q11430321|WD=Q11430321|lt=external}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|1=UNKNOWN}} for contributions of private grain to the garrison in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}, is said either to have lived in Sōsa District or to have retired there after stepping down as {{ill|lt=negi|Q11594490|WD=Q11594490|lt=chief negi}} in old age. These traditions show ties with {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380}}, and ties between Katori Jingū and the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}} are also discussedKenichi Tanigawa, ''Nihon no Kamigami: Jinja to Seichi 11'', Hakusuisha, 2000, ISBN 4-560-02511-8, pp.265-267. In {{ill|lt=Shōhei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei}} 24 ({{ill|lt=1369|Q6211|WD=Q6211}}), the shrine buildings were burned down and rebuilt by the {{ill|lt=Chiba clan|Q2975237|WD=Q2975237}}. Thereafter, it was protected by the Chiba clan, but declined along with that clan’s weakening. In October {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precincts === Behind the shrine buildings on the left is a sacred cedar designated by the city as a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}}. === Access === * {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071}} / {{ill|lt=Sōbu Main Line|Q1191364|WD=Q1191364}}, get off at {{ill|lt=Yōkaichiba Station|Q5363766|WD=Q5363766}}, then 25 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おいおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oio Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oio Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:匝瑳市の建築物]] [[Category:匝瑳市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県匝瑳市生尾75', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:老尾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610638}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Oio Shrine | 画像=[[File:老尾神社 拝殿.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367|1=Sōsa}} Oio 75 | 祭神=Asahiko-no-Mikoto | 別名=Sōsa Daimyōjin | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7 | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774|1=August 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Oio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oio Shrine''' (おいおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Oio, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621|1=Sōsa District, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367|1=Sōsa}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small shrine), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, child deity of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}, deity of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}}. Also enshrined are {{ill|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o|Q9010200|WD=Q9010200|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o-no-Mikoto and Iwatsutsuno-me-no-Mikoto|1=Iwa-tsutsu-no'o}} and {{ill|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|Q478504|WD=Q478504|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}. There is also a theory that {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689|1=Mononobe no Kogoto}} is enshrined. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Oio Shrine, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621|1=Sōsa District, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}},” and ranked as a small shrine. Within {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}, the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the territory of the {{ill|lt=Shimotsu-unakami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11361123|WD=Q11361123|1=Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko}} besides this shrine was {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}}, and this shrine was treated as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} of Sōsa District in Shimōsa. The district where the shrine is located, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621|1=Sōsa District, Chiba}}, is said to have been established due to the military merits of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689|1=Mononobe no Kogoto}} in subduing the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bandō|1=UNKNOWN}} region; traditions also convey deep ties with the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, including three generations of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe Sōsa clan|Q24864171|WD=Q24864171|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa clan|1=Mononobe no Sōsa clan}} - {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Ashitsugi|Q11570688|WD=Q11570688|lt=Ashitsugi|1=Ashitsugi}}, {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Kuma-i|Q11570687|WD=Q11570687|lt=Kuma-i|1=Kuma-i}}, and Suemori - serving as commanders guarding the region, together with {{ill|lt=Shita District|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034|1=Shida district}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}}. Also, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōsa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} exists as a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}}, and rebuilding this {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrine|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha|1=Setsumatsusha}} is said to have formerly been a duty of Sōsa District. Furthermore, this shrine’s priestly family was the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori no Muraji Ioshima|1=UNKNOWN}}, who was granted {{ill|lt=external rank|Q11430321|WD=Q11430321|lt=external|1=gei}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|1=UNKNOWN}} for contributions of private grain to the garrison in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}, is said either to have lived in Sōsa District or to have retired there after stepping down as {{ill|lt=negi|Q11594490|WD=Q11594490|lt=chief negi|1=Negi}} in old age. These traditions show ties with {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}}, and ties between Katori Jingū and the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}} are also discussedKenichi Tanigawa, ''Nihon no Kamigami: Jinja to Seichi 11'', Hakusuisha, 2000, ISBN 4-560-02511-8, pp.265-267. In {{ill|lt=Shōhei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei|1=Shōhei}} 24 ({{ill|lt=1369|Q6211|WD=Q6211|1=1369}}), the shrine buildings were burned down and rebuilt by the {{ill|lt=Chiba clan|Q2975237|WD=Q2975237|1=Chiba clan}}. Thereafter, it was protected by the Chiba clan, but declined along with that clan’s weakening. In October {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Precincts === Behind the shrine buildings on the left is a sacred cedar designated by the city as a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}}. === Access === * {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071|1=East Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Sōbu Main Line|Q1191364|WD=Q1191364|1=Sōbu Main Line}}, get off at {{ill|lt=Yōkaichiba Station|Q5363766|WD=Q5363766|1=Yōkaichiba Station}}, then 25 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おいおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oio Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oio Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:匝瑳市の建築物]] [[Category:匝瑳市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おいおじんじゃ == postal code (P281) == * 289-2104https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9040005011815 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県匝瑳市生尾75', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:老尾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610638}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Oio Shrine | 画像=[[File:老尾神社 拝殿.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367|1=Sōsa}} Oio 75 | 祭神=Asahiko-no-Mikoto | 別名=Sōsa Daimyōjin | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7 | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774|1=August 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Oio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oio Shrine''' (おいおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Oio, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621|1=Sōsa District, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367|1=Sōsa}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small shrine), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, child deity of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}, deity of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}}. Also enshrined are {{ill|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o|Q9010200|WD=Q9010200|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o-no-Mikoto and Iwatsutsuno-me-no-Mikoto|1=Iwa-tsutsu-no'o}} and {{ill|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|Q478504|WD=Q478504|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}. There is also a theory that {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689|1=Mononobe no Kogoto}} is enshrined. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Oio Shrine, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621|1=Sōsa District, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}},” and ranked as a small shrine. Within {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}, the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the territory of the {{ill|lt=Shimotsu-unakami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11361123|WD=Q11361123|1=Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko}} besides this shrine was {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}}, and this shrine was treated as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} of Sōsa District in Shimōsa. The district where the shrine is located, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621|1=Sōsa District, Chiba}}, is said to have been established due to the military merits of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689|1=Mononobe no Kogoto}} in subduing the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bandō|1=UNKNOWN}} region; traditions also convey deep ties with the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, including three generations of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe Sōsa clan|Q24864171|WD=Q24864171|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa clan|1=Mononobe no Sōsa clan}} - {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Ashitsugi|Q11570688|WD=Q11570688|lt=Ashitsugi|1=Ashitsugi}}, {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Kuma-i|Q11570687|WD=Q11570687|lt=Kuma-i|1=Kuma-i}}, and Suemori - serving as commanders guarding the region, together with {{ill|lt=Shita District|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034|1=Shida district}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}}. Also, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōsa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} exists as a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}}, and rebuilding this {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrine|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha|1=Setsumatsusha}} is said to have formerly been a duty of Sōsa District. Furthermore, this shrine’s priestly family was the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori no Muraji Ioshima|1=UNKNOWN}}, who was granted {{ill|lt=external rank|Q11430321|WD=Q11430321|lt=external|1=gei}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|1=UNKNOWN}} for contributions of private grain to the garrison in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}, is said either to have lived in Sōsa District or to have retired there after stepping down as {{ill|lt=negi|Q11594490|WD=Q11594490|lt=chief negi|1=Negi}} in old age. These traditions show ties with {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}}, and ties between Katori Jingū and the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}} are also discussedKenichi Tanigawa, ''Nihon no Kamigami: Jinja to Seichi 11'', Hakusuisha, 2000, ISBN 4-560-02511-8, pp.265-267. In {{ill|lt=Shōhei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei|1=Shōhei}} 24 ({{ill|lt=1369|Q6211|WD=Q6211|1=1369}}), the shrine buildings were burned down and rebuilt by the {{ill|lt=Chiba clan|Q2975237|WD=Q2975237|1=Chiba clan}}. Thereafter, it was protected by the Chiba clan, but declined along with that clan’s weakening. In October {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Precincts === Behind the shrine buildings on the left is a sacred cedar designated by the city as a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}}. === Access === * {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071|1=East Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Sōbu Main Line|Q1191364|WD=Q1191364|1=Sōbu Main Line}}, get off at {{ill|lt=Yōkaichiba Station|Q5363766|WD=Q5363766|1=Yōkaichiba Station}}, then 25 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おいおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oio Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oio Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:匝瑳市の建築物]] [[Category:匝瑳市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おいおじんじゃ == postal code (P281) == * 289-2104https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9040005011815 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県匝瑳市生尾75', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:老尾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610638}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Oio Shrine | 画像=[[File:老尾神社 拝殿.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367|1=Sōsa}} Oio 75 | 祭神=Asahiko-no-Mikoto | 別名=Sōsa Daimyōjin | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7 | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774|1=August 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Oio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oio Shrine''' (おいおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Oio, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621|1=Sōsa District, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367|1=Sōsa}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small shrine), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, child deity of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}, deity of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}}. Also enshrined are {{ill|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o|Q9010200|WD=Q9010200|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o-no-Mikoto and Iwatsutsuno-me-no-Mikoto|1=Iwa-tsutsu-no'o}} and {{ill|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|Q478504|WD=Q478504|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}. There is also a theory that {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689|1=Mononobe no Kogoto}} is enshrined. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Oio Shrine, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621|1=Sōsa District, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}},” and ranked as a small shrine. Within {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}, the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the territory of the {{ill|lt=Shimotsu-unakami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11361123|WD=Q11361123|1=Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko}} besides this shrine was {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}}, and this shrine was treated as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} of Sōsa District in Shimōsa. The district where the shrine is located, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621|1=Sōsa District, Chiba}}, is said to have been established due to the military merits of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689|1=Mononobe no Kogoto}} in subduing the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bandō|1=UNKNOWN}} region; traditions also convey deep ties with the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, including three generations of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe Sōsa clan|Q24864171|WD=Q24864171|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa clan|1=Mononobe no Sōsa clan}} - {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Ashitsugi|Q11570688|WD=Q11570688|lt=Ashitsugi|1=Ashitsugi}}, {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Kuma-i|Q11570687|WD=Q11570687|lt=Kuma-i|1=Kuma-i}}, and Suemori - serving as commanders guarding the region, together with {{ill|lt=Shita District|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034|1=Shida district}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}}. Also, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōsa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} exists as a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}}, and rebuilding this {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrine|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha|1=Setsumatsusha}} is said to have formerly been a duty of Sōsa District. Furthermore, this shrine’s priestly family was the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori no Muraji Ioshima|1=UNKNOWN}}, who was granted {{ill|lt=external rank|Q11430321|WD=Q11430321|lt=external|1=gei}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|1=UNKNOWN}} for contributions of private grain to the garrison in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}, is said either to have lived in Sōsa District or to have retired there after stepping down as {{ill|lt=negi|Q11594490|WD=Q11594490|lt=chief negi|1=Negi}} in old age. These traditions show ties with {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}}, and ties between Katori Jingū and the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}} are also discussedKenichi Tanigawa, ''Nihon no Kamigami: Jinja to Seichi 11'', Hakusuisha, 2000, ISBN 4-560-02511-8, pp.265-267. In {{ill|lt=Shōhei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei|1=Shōhei}} 24 ({{ill|lt=1369|Q6211|WD=Q6211|1=1369}}), the shrine buildings were burned down and rebuilt by the {{ill|lt=Chiba clan|Q2975237|WD=Q2975237|1=Chiba clan}}. Thereafter, it was protected by the Chiba clan, but declined along with that clan’s weakening. In October {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Precincts === Behind the shrine buildings on the left is a sacred cedar designated by the city as a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}}. === Access === * {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071|1=East Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Sōbu Main Line|Q1191364|WD=Q1191364|1=Sōbu Main Line}}, get off at {{ill|lt=Yōkaichiba Station|Q5363766|WD=Q5363766|1=Yōkaichiba Station}}, then 25 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おいおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oio Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oio Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:匝瑳市の建築物]] [[Category:匝瑳市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おいおじんじゃ == postal code (P281) == * 289-2104https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9040005011815 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県匝瑳市生尾75', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:老尾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610638}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Oio Shrine | 画像=[[File:老尾神社 拝殿.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367|1=Sōsa}} Oio 75 | 祭神=Asahiko-no-Mikoto | 別名=Sōsa Daimyōjin | 創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7 | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774|1=August 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Oio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oio Shrine''' (おいおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Oio, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621|1=Sōsa District, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Sōsa|Q858367|WD=Q858367|1=Sōsa}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small shrine), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, child deity of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}, deity of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}}. Also enshrined are {{ill|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o|Q9010200|WD=Q9010200|lt=Iwatsutsuno-o-no-Mikoto and Iwatsutsuno-me-no-Mikoto|1=Iwa-tsutsu-no'o}} and {{ill|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|Q478504|WD=Q478504|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}. There is also a theory that {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689|1=Mononobe no Kogoto}} is enshrined. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in year 7 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Oio Shrine, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621|1=Sōsa District, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}},” and ranked as a small shrine. Within {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}, the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the territory of the {{ill|lt=Shimotsu-unakami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11361123|WD=Q11361123|1=Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko}} besides this shrine was {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}}, and this shrine was treated as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} of Sōsa District in Shimōsa. The district where the shrine is located, {{ill|lt=Sōsa District|Q7667621|WD=Q7667621|1=Sōsa District, Chiba}}, is said to have been established due to the military merits of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Ogoto|Q11570689|WD=Q11570689|1=Mononobe no Kogoto}} in subduing the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bandō|1=UNKNOWN}} region; traditions also convey deep ties with the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, including three generations of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe Sōsa clan|Q24864171|WD=Q24864171|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa clan|1=Mononobe no Sōsa clan}} - {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Ashitsugi|Q11570688|WD=Q11570688|lt=Ashitsugi|1=Ashitsugi}}, {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Sōsa no Kuma-i|Q11570687|WD=Q11570687|lt=Kuma-i|1=Kuma-i}}, and Suemori - serving as commanders guarding the region, together with {{ill|lt=Shita District|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034|1=Shida district}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}}. Also, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōsa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} exists as a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}}, and rebuilding this {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrine|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha|1=Setsumatsusha}} is said to have formerly been a duty of Sōsa District. Furthermore, this shrine’s priestly family was the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katori no Muraji Ioshima|1=UNKNOWN}}, who was granted {{ill|lt=external rank|Q11430321|WD=Q11430321|lt=external|1=gei}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|1=UNKNOWN}} for contributions of private grain to the garrison in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}, is said either to have lived in Sōsa District or to have retired there after stepping down as {{ill|lt=negi|Q11594490|WD=Q11594490|lt=chief negi|1=Negi}} in old age. These traditions show ties with {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|WD=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}}, and ties between Katori Jingū and the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}} are also discussedKenichi Tanigawa, ''Nihon no Kamigami: Jinja to Seichi 11'', Hakusuisha, 2000, ISBN 4-560-02511-8, pp.265-267. In {{ill|lt=Shōhei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei|1=Shōhei}} 24 ({{ill|lt=1369|Q6211|WD=Q6211|1=1369}}), the shrine buildings were burned down and rebuilt by the {{ill|lt=Chiba clan|Q2975237|WD=Q2975237|1=Chiba clan}}. Thereafter, it was protected by the Chiba clan, but declined along with that clan’s weakening. In October {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Precincts === Behind the shrine buildings on the left is a sacred cedar designated by the city as a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}}. === Access === * {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071|1=East Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Sōbu Main Line|Q1191364|WD=Q1191364|1=Sōbu Main Line}}, get off at {{ill|lt=Yōkaichiba Station|Q5363766|WD=Q5363766|1=Yōkaichiba Station}}, then 25 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おいおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oio Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oio Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:匝瑳市の建築物]] [[Category:匝瑳市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]
